templateTitle: The Compose Logbook — Weekly Ship Report
tagline: One page, every class. Sketch it, ship it, log the diff — watch your app grow week over week.
howToUse: Duplicate LAST week's filled-in copy and rename it (e.g. “Logbook — Class 08”). You fill this by DIFFING against last week, so keep the old one open beside it.
Fill top-to-bottom in 10–15 minutes. Phrases, checkmarks, and a sketch — not paragraphs. If a section takes more than ~2 minutes, write less.
Each section has ONE job, so you only type things once: the Diff is WHAT changed · the Design Log is WHY · the Plan is NEXT. Carried-over items copy verbatim — don't reword them.
Paste a real screenshot / emulator capture where you can; a quick boxed wireframe is fine otherwise. Always note light/dark and which UI state is showing.
First class, or missed a week? Skip Burndown and the Diff's ~/- lines — list what exists now as all “+” lines and just fill the Plan. You'll diff for real next week.
Write in Compose vocabulary on purpose (NavKey, ViewModel, StateFlow, sealed UI state, modifier order) — filling this page is your weekly review of the course.

metadataFields.label: App name
metadataFields.hint: your semester app + 3–5 words on what it does

metadataFields.label: This week's theme
metadataFields.hint: ONE phrase, e.g. “wire the data layer”

metadataFields.label: Build
metadataFields.hint: version bump so progress shows: v0.6 → v0.7

metadataFields.label: Repo / commit
metadataFields.hint: link this week's commit or PR — the proof behind your Diff

metadataFields.label: Energy
metadataFields.hint: one emoji for your build mood

sections.title: Burndown — last class's promises
sections.purpose: Read back what you committed to at the END of last class and mark what actually happened. Closes the loop so “progress” is verifiable, not just claimed.
prompts: Paste each commitment from last week's “Plan for next class” into a row, then mark it Done / Partial / Carried / Dropped.
If it isn't Done, add a one-phrase why. Carried rows copy VERBATIM into this week's Plan below — don't reword them.
sections.example: (1) Add RideDetailScreen via Nav3 — Done. (2) Wire RideRepository to emit a sealed RideUiState — Partial (Error branch still a TODO). (3) Add a “seats left” Slider — Carried (ran out of time after the nav refactor).
sampleRows: Wire the data layer to a sealed UiState

sections.title: The Diff — what shipped since last class
sections.purpose: One headline plus the delta only, in git-diff style. The markers structurally stop you from re-listing old work.
prompts: Top line — “Shipped this week”: in ONE sentence, what a classmate reopening your app would notice is new.
Then 3–6 delta lines, each prefixed:  + added · ~ changed existing · − removed/redid  (just like a git diff).
The WHAT only — save every “why” for the Design Log so you don't type it twice.
sections.example: Shipped: the ride-detail screen is live.  + Added RideDetailScreen (NavKey RideDetail(rideId)).  + Added RideRepository emitting a sealed RideUiState.  ~ RideListViewModel now exposes StateFlow<RideUiState> instead of a raw List.  − Removed the hard-coded sampleRides list from the Composable.

sections.title: Design decision log
sections.purpose: The design steps and the WHY behind your choices — the trade-offs you made on purpose. At least one UI/layout decision AND one architecture/state decision each week.
prompts: Log each real decision. The Why must name a concrete force — rotation, screen size, scannability, testability, back-stack depth, recomposition cost. “It's cleaner” is not a Why.
If you can't name what you gave up in the Trade-off column, you didn't make a decision — you just coded. Write “see Design Log” elsewhere instead of repeating it.
sections.example: UI — Gave RideDetail its own Nav3 screen | Why: a map + notes + driver card is too much for a list row | Rejected: an inline-expanding Card (it pushed siblings off-screen in the Playground) | Trade-off: one extra back-stack entry, but the list stays scannable.
sampleRows: RideDetail gets its own Nav3 screen
Too much content for a list row
Hoist search text into the ViewModel

sections.title: Architecture & state (in Compose terms)
sections.purpose: Snapshot your app's plumbing in the course's vocabulary. Doubles as a weekly self-test.
prompts: Fill the table: how did Navigation, State, and the Data layer change this week — and what's still TODO? If a row just repeats a Design Log decision, write “see Design Log”.
Then mark your main screen's sealed UI-state branches below: which are REAL vs still faked.
sections.example: Navigation: before [RideList] → after [RideList, RideDetail(id)]; TODO: pass rideId into the detail ViewModel cleanly. State: raw List → StateFlow<RideUiState> via collectAsStateWithLifecycle(). Data: hard-coded list → RideRepository emitting the sealed state; TODO: a real source.
sections.checklistLabel: My main screen's sealed UI state — mark each branch (✅ real · ⬜ still TODO):

sections.title: UI this week — sketch + the pieces behind it
sections.purpose: Show the actual UI that changed plus the exact Compose building blocks behind it. Reinforces “order matters” and the light/dark @Preview habit.
prompts: Paste a screenshot / emulator capture OR sketch a boxed wireframe of the screen(s) you touched. Label the containers (Column / Row / Box / LazyColumn / Card / Scaffold) and caption it: screen name + UI state shown + light/dark.
Write ONE modifier chain IN ORDER and a one-line note on why the order matters (size-affecting vs draw-only) — or what breaks if you reorder it.
Then tick the Material 3 pieces you used this week.
sections.checklistLabel: Material 3 pieces I used this week (★ anything used for the first time):
checklistItems: Verified in BOTH light and dark @Preview

sections.title: What works right now — the demo
sections.purpose: Prove the build runs: the 30-second demo you could give today, with an honesty flag so a slick screenshot can't oversell a stub.
prompts: Write the tap-path you'd demo live — what you can do this week that you couldn't last week.
Disclose what's faked / stubbed / hard-coded behind the demo, and where it runs (device/emulator vs @Preview only).
sections.example: Demo: launch → RideList shows 3 rides → tap one → Nav3 pushes RideDetail(id) → ~0.5s spinner → Success renders route + seats → Back returns with scroll kept. Runs on the Pixel 6 emulator. Faked: the 3 rides are hard-coded in FakeRideRepository.
sections.checklistLabel: Green checks — tick only what genuinely passes right now:
checklistItems: Builds & runs on emulator/device with no crash
The new feature is tap-through demoable
Pushed — commit link pasted in the header above

sections.title: Blockers & open questions
sections.purpose: Surface what's stuck and the exact questions you want answered — specific enough to act on in class. Triage, not failure.
prompts: List 1–3 blockers. Name the Composable / ViewModel / concept, rate severity, say whether you're Blocked or just Slowed, and what you've already tried.
Star ★ the ONE question most worth class time.
sections.example: Blocked (High): can't pass rideId into RideDetailViewModel cleanly — through the NavKey or a SavedStateHandle? Tried reading it off the back-stack key directly, felt wrong. ★ Top question: what's the idiomatic way to get a NavKey's argument into a ViewModel?
sampleRows: read it off the back-stack key — felt wrong
checklistItems: Stuck > 30 min — I want you to look at this with me in class

sections.title: Plan for next class — commitments
sections.purpose: Pre-write next week's work as small, verifiable cards so you start from momentum, not a blank page. Carried items from Burndown flow in here.
prompts: Commit to 1–3 small, demoable cards. Pull in anything marked Carried above (verbatim).
Write a one-line done-test for the ★ MUST-DO: “done = I can tap ___ and see ___ in the Success state.”
Note what you'll prototype in the Compose Playground first, and draft next week's commit headline now (imperative).
sections.example: ★ MUST-DO: wire the Error branch + a Retry Button (done = kill the network, see the error UI, tap Retry, see Success). Stretch: add the seats-left Slider [carried]. Playground first: the request-form layout. Commit headline: “feat: add error+retry to RideList”.
checklistItems: ★ MUST-DO card — the one thing that, if only one ships, ships
★ MUST-DO done-test:  done = I can tap ____ and see ____
Prototype in the Compose Playground first  →

sections.title: Confidence dials (1–5)
sections.purpose: A fast, honest self-rating to track growth over the semester and surface what to fix next. Just tick — then name your lowest axis as next week's focus.
prompts: Mark one box per row (1 = shaky · 5 = could teach it).
Your lowest axis is next week's focus — circle it.

sections.title: Brag, bug & codename (the fun close)
sections.purpose: End on a high note so the page stays human and motivating to read back later.
prompts: 🏆 Brag: one moment you felt like a real Android dev this week.
🐞 Bug of the week: the funniest/dumbest bug you hit and the fix — one line.
💡 Learned + 🏷️ Codename: one post-it-worthy line of Compose wisdom, then a fun release codename for this build.
sections.example: 🏆 Brag: the list updated itself after the repo emitted a new state — zero refresh code, declarative UI finally clicked. 🐞 Bug: blank screen for 20 min — my Column wasn't scrollable; swapped to LazyColumn. 💡 Learned: clip the shape BEFORE you paint the background. 🏷️ v0.7 — “Carpool Lane”.

footer: Small commits, every class. Duplicate this page next week and diff against today — that growing stack of logbooks IS your app shipping. 🚀
